UniCredit Strengthens Alliance with Alpha Bank Across Europe
data.description:
(Alliance News) – Foreign markets remain at the heart of UniCredit's growth strategy, as confirmed by the joint executive committee meeting with Alpha Bank in Munich, attended by the top management of both groups and reported Thursday by MF-Milano Finanza.

The alliance is rooted in the Greek bank's recovery path following its state rescue through the Hellenic Financial Stability Fund during the 2009-2010 crisis, followed by a deep restructuring that led to a drastic reduction in non-performing loans and a return to solid profits.

The partnership between UniCredit and Alpha began in 2023 with the merger of their respective subsidiaries in Romania and the launch of the AlphaLife joint venture for insurance and pension products, 51% controlled by the Italian group.

The financial project came to fruition in recent weeks with the European Central Bank's authorization, allowing UniCredit to increase its stake in Alpha Bank from 10% to 29.9%. The Munich meeting reviewed achievements between 2024 and 2025 and set a new agenda to create further opportunities for European clients, with the ambition to build one of the continent's most integrated cross-border banking collaborations.

The cooperation is strengthened by the presence of major groups active in Italy, Germany, and Greece—markets connected by value chains, trade exchanges, and cross-investments in energy, infrastructure, industry, logistics, and consumer goods.

The joint platform aims to combine UniCredit's scale in Italy and Germany with Alpha's strong presence in Greece and Cyprus, supporting regional growth plans.

UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel emphasized that “our partnership with Alpha demonstrates the strength of cooperation in fostering development and innovation in Europe.”

By combining UniCredit's scale and digital capabilities with Alpha's local strength, we are creating a platform able to offer cutting-edge solutions and drive growth in key markets,” he added, noting that the federal model “allows us to respect national identities while building an integrated network that generates value for clients, shareholders, and communities.”

Alpha Bank CEO Vassilis Psaltis recalled Germany's historic role for Greek businesses, explaining that “together with UniCredit we are working to offer clients new growth opportunities beyond national borders,” and that the Munich meetings laid the groundwork for more targeted involvement in 2026.

The initiative ultimately confirms UniCredit's focus on Central and Balkan Europe, areas that Orcel aims to revitalize through targeted M&A operations, while growth plans in Italy remain temporarily on hold.

By Antonio Di Giorgio, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Taiwan Defense Stocks Waver as Markets Weigh Budget Boost
data.description:
By Jason Chau


Taiwan defense stocks retreated Thursday morning, giving back gains fueled by hopes of higher government spending--an outlook analysts say is uncertain.

Shares of defense-related companies had climbed in Taipei over the past week on expectations the government would boost its budget amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

Taiwanese President President Lai Ching-te's pledge to introduce a $40 billion supplementary defense budget saw the sector rising Wednesday, with the momentum quickly fading.

Aerospace Industrial Development Corp., Magnate Technology and Aero Win Technology were down 4%-6% at midday, reversing nearly 10% gains from the previous session.

The benchmark Taiex index was last up 0.3%.

Analysts say the early-week gains were driven by an op-ed Lai wrote for the Washington Post outlining his plan for additional defense spending.

Lai's opinion piece "shows there is a stronger commitment to increase defense spending," said Allan von Mehren, chief analyst at Danske Bank, attributing the op-ed as a catalyst for the rally.

Eyck Freymann of the Hoover Institution at Stanford University said Taiwan needs major investments in its drone supply chain, counter-drone technologies and strategic stockpiles of key industrial inputs.

Macquarie analyst Jeffrey Ohlweiler said drone makers, including Aerospace Industrial Development, Evergreen Aviation Technologies and Thunder Tiger, would benefit, along with shipbuilders such as CSBC and Lungteh Shipbuilding which also stand to gain.


Write to Jason Chau at jason.chau@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-27-25 0109ET

China Taiping Insurance shares fall on concerns about exposure to Hong Kong fire
data.description:
HONG KONG (Reuters) -Shares in China Taiping Insurance Holdings dropped as much as 8% on Thursday on concerns about its potential liability exposure to a Hong Kong apartment complex where a huge fire has killed at least 44 people and left nearly 300 missing.

According to publicly available minutes of meetings of registered owners of Wang Fuk Court, members approved the continuation of insurance coverage for the housing complex with China Taiping Insurance (Hong Kong) Ltd in December 2024.

The policy period runs from January 1, 2025, to December 31, 2026, the records show. 

China Taiping Insurance Holdings and its unit China Taiping Insurance (Hong Kong) did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment. 

The tightly packed complex in the northern Tai Po district in Hong Kong has 2,000 apartments in eight blocks that are home to more than 4,600 people in a city struggling with chronic shortages of affordable housing.

China Taiping Insurance shares on Thursday dropped as much as 8.1% to the lowest level since October 24. The stock trimmed losses later to be down 1.4%, lagging a 0.3% gain in the benchmark Hang Seng Index.

State-owned China Taiping provides a host of general insurance services - from marine hull to property insurance.     

(Reporting by Donny Kwok, Claire Fu, Selena Li, and Ziyi Tang; Ediitng by Sumeet Chatterjee and Lincoln Feast.)

JP Morgan shifts outlook on Fed rate cut to December
data.description:
(Reuters) -J.P. Morgan said on Wednesday it now expects the U.S. Federal Reserve to deliver a 25-basis-point rate cut in December, reversing its earlier call that policymakers would stay on hold until January.

The shift follows comments from key officials, including New York Fed President and FOMC Vice Chair John Williams, that hinted at an earlier move, signaling the central bank's likely stance to markets.

Goldman Sachs said in a separate note on Wednesday that with no other major data release scheduled before December 9-10, "the September jobs report may have sealed a 25 basis points cut at the December 9-10 FOMC meeting." 

"While the next FOMC meeting remains a close call, we now believe the latest round of Fedspeak tilts the odds toward the Committee deciding to cut rates in two weeks from today," said J.P. Morgan's chief U.S. economist Michael Feroli in a note.

"We're back to looking for a final cut in January," Feroli said.

Traders are pricing in a nearly 85% chance of a quarter-point interest rate cut in December, as per the CME FedWatch tool.

Earlier this month J.P. Morgan had withdrawn its forecast for a December cut following the September jobs report that was delayed due to the Government shutdown.

(Reporting by Kanchana Chakravarty in Bengaluru)
